titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Minister Resident in Saudi Arabia ( (Moose))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister Resident in Saudi Arabia ( Moose )
div.p:
128. In view of King Ibn Saud’s recent urgent appeal to the American and British Governments for aid, it is considered essential that the King be informed as soon as possible that both Governments have reached agreement on the joint aid program worked out recently in Cairo. You should, therefore, arrange to visit the King as soon as practicable to apprise him thereof. You should also invite your British colleague to accompany you in order that this information may be conveyed to the King jointly if possible. At the time of your visit you should convey to the King substantially the information contained in the following lettered paragraphs:
[Here follow paragraphs (a) through (g), which were superseded by those contained in telegram 149, July 26, 5 p.m., printed on page 719.]
(h) In view of the joint supply program which has been worked out with the consequent assurance that Saudi Arabia’s requirements
will be met, it is hoped that the King will consider it unnecessary to use, and will refrain from using, for supply purchases the dollar funds being made available to him by the Arabian American Oil Company as a result of his recent request to the Company for assistance. It should be pointed out to the King that if he does use these funds for supply purchases and reimburses the oil company, as has been suggested, out of the 40 percent profit accruing from the 10 million riyal purchase transaction whereby riyals are to be sold to commercial firms in Saudi Arabia, the Government of Saudi Arabia will lose the substantial profit to be derived from the contemplated purchase and sale of gold.

list.item: In connection with the matter discussed in lettered paragraph (h) above, you are advised for your own information that officials of the Arabian American Oil Company here agree that, in view of the joint supply program which has been outlined to them, it will be both unnecessary and undesirable for the King to draw upon the funds which they have placed at his disposal as an emergency measure without prior consultation with the Department. The oil company, therefore, is advising one of its representatives in Dhahran to fly to Jidda to discuss this matter with you, if possible, before you visit the King.

It is suggested that Gunter, Treasury representative from Cairo, accompany you on your visit to the King to be of such assistance as may be desirable in the technical explanation of the supply program and the budget. The British are making the same suggestion with reference to Mr. Bailey,Deputy Director General of the Middle East Supply Center. or other suitable representative, accompanying Jordan.

The foregoing paragraphs 1 through 3 have been agreed upon in consultation with British officials in Washington. They are being telegraphed by them immediately to London with a request that if the British Government concurs, it will forthwith issue identical instructions to the British Minister in Jidda. You should not therefore make arrangements to visit the King until your British colleague has received his instructions.
